case_name: Leaverton v. Cascade Surgical Partners, PLLC

summary.brief: Leaverton v. Cascade Surgical Partners, PLLC. Medical malpractice case involving oncology.

evidence_index.text: Lajuana Leaverton suffered from a multinodular goiter (enlargements of her thyroid) and hyperthyroidism (excessive secretions of her thyroid).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Conroy performed a subtotal thyroidectomy on Ms. Leaverton in late November 2003.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: After the surgery, Ms. Leaverton had high-pitched, harsh sounding respiration, called stridor.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Both expressed opinions that the cause of the injury was the use of electrocautery too close, within 0.5 centimeters, of the left recurrent laryngeal nerve.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: The trial judge concluded that the failure to introduce opinions on the standard for a general surgeon was fatal to the patient’s suit and he summarily dismissed her complaint.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: We conclude that the expert’s familiarity with the disease and its management, surgically, is sufficient to submit the matter to jury and we therefore reverse the summary dismissal and remand for trial.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: Dr. Chan has practiced as an otolaryngologist for over 34 years. At one point in his career, he was Chief of Otolaryngology at Providence Hospital in Seattle, Washington.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: Dr. Souliere, too, is familiar with thyroid disease and the surgical procedure of thyroidectomy. He is a board certified otolaryngologist.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: We reverse the summary judgment and remand for trial.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Lajuana Leaverton suffered from a multinodular goiter and hyperthyroidism. She was referred for surgical treatment.

focused_exam.neck: Enlarged thyroid with several palpable nodules

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Multinodular goiter with hyperthyroidism requiring surgical intervention

requestables.name: Surgical Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with Dr. Conroy regarding surgical options for thyroid condition

reveal.clinical_significance: Decision based on anatomy and risk of nerve injury
reveal.decision_impact: Informed consent obtained for subtotal thyroidectomy

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with multinodular goiter and hyperthyroidism

key_events.event: Referral to Dr. Conroy for surgical evaluation

timeline_phases.name: Surgical Procedure
timeline_phases.description: Subtotal thyroidectomy performed by Dr. Conroy

timeline_phases.name: Postoperative Complications
timeline_phases.description: Patient develops stridor and vocal cord paralysis

decision_points.clinical_context: During subtotal thyroidectomy, identification of recurrent laryngeal nerve
decision_points.prompt: How should the surgeon proceed to avoid nerve injury?

options.description: Use blunt dissection to identify and protect the recurrent laryngeal nerve
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard practice to avoid nerve damage

options.description: Use electrocautery near the nerve without clear identification

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant used electrocautery too close to the nerve

expected_action_court.description: Blunt dissection should have been used to avoid nerve injury
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony on standard surgical practice

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Use of electrocautery within 0.5 cm of the nerve is below standard of care
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Blunt dissection to identify and protect the nerve
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed stridor and vocal cord paralysis

key_findings: Reversal of summary judgment and remand for trial

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Use of electrocautery too close to nerve
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Blunt dissection would have avoided nerve injury
